source_snapshot.note: Snapshot is informative while draft. Recompute immediately before freeze.

design.condition_explanation: Three general-purpose models each run M1 and M3; BTGenBot-2 adds one M2 condition.

missions.title: Husky single-point temperature reading

complexity.label: simple
complexity.rationale: Navigate and sense at one supplied point with no route derivation.

paraphrases.text: Drive the Husky to inspection point P1 and log the temperature there.
Go to P1 with the ground robot, then record one temperature measurement.
Take a temperature reading at the marked ground inspection point P1.

constraints: Use point P1 in the map frame.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not replace P1 with a guessed location.

expected.equivalent_output_rule: One map-frame waypoint at P1 and one temperature log action is acceptable.

reference_behavior: Parse or otherwise represent the supplied point.
Navigate to P1.
Log one temperature measurement after arrival.

missions.title: BlueBoat single-point water temperature reading

complexity.label: simple
complexity.rationale: Navigate and sense at one supplied water point.

paraphrases.text: Take the BlueBoat to water station W1 and record the water temperature.
Sail to W1 and log one water-temperature measurement there.
Measure the water temperature at the marked BlueBoat station W1.

constraints: Use water station W1 in the map frame.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not use the Husky ground tree or its navigation action name.

expected.equivalent_output_rule: One map-frame waypoint at W1 and one water-temperature log action is acceptable.

reference_behavior: Use the BlueBoat navigation interface.
Navigate to W1.
Log one water-temperature measurement after arrival.
missions.blocking_requirement: Implement and validate the BlueBoat capability model, navigation adapter, temperature interface, tree XML, and metadata.

missions.title: Husky supplied route with temperature logging

complexity.label: simple
complexity.rationale: A supplied three-point route requires repetition but no route derivation.

paraphrases.text: Visit ground waypoints G1, G2, and G3 in that order and log temperature at each.
Drive the Husky through G1 to G3 and record one temperature value at every point.
Follow the supplied route G1–G2–G3, measuring temperature after each arrival.

constraints: Preserve the order G1, G2, G3.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not omit a supplied point or reorder the route.

expected.equivalent_output_rule: The exact supplied coordinates and order must be preserved.

reference_behavior: Represent all three supplied points in order.
Repeat navigation for each point.
Log temperature after each successful arrival.

missions.title: Husky route photography at a fixed distance interval

complexity.label: medium
complexity.rationale: A supplied route combines navigation with distance-triggered photography and explicit output settings.

paraphrases.text: Follow inspection route R1 with the Husky and take a photograph every 10 metres.
Drive along R1 and save route photos at 10 m travel intervals.
Document the supplied ground route R1 by capturing an image after each 10 metres travelled.

constraints: Write images to the evaluation output directory.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not change the requested interval to the tree default.

expected.equivalent_output_rule: The supplied route and 10 m interval are fixed; equivalent file naming is acceptable if auditable.

reference_behavior: Follow all R1 waypoints in order.
Monitor travelled odometry distance while navigating.
Capture and save RGB images at approximately 10 m intervals.

missions.title: BlueBoat supplied shoreline stations with temperature logging

complexity.label: medium
complexity.rationale: A supplied water route combines repeated navigation and sensing with route and geofence constraints.

paraphrases.text: Take the BlueBoat through shoreline stations B1 to B4 and log water temperature at each station.
Follow the supplied B1–B4 water route, recording temperature after every arrival.
Survey the four marked shoreline points in order and save one water-temperature reading per point.

forbidden_assumptions: Do not use ground-only navigation interfaces.

expected.equivalent_output_rule: The supplied coordinates and station order must be preserved.

reference_behavior: Use the BlueBoat navigation interface.
Visit B1 through B4 in order within the water geofence.
Log water temperature after each arrival.
missions.blocking_requirement: Implement and validate the BlueBoat capability model, navigation adapter, temperature interface, tree XML, and metadata.

missions.title: Husky context-derived temperature route

complexity.label: medium
complexity.rationale: Three measurement points must be derived from a mapped zone using several context sources.

paraphrases.text: Choose three valid points in the northern monitoring zone and log temperature at each with the Husky.
Use the map context to plan three ground temperature readings across the north zone.
Sample temperature at three well-spaced locations inside the marked northern area.

constraints: Keep every point inside the north zone.
Spread points across the zone rather than clustering them.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not use geographic coordinates directly as map-frame x,y values.

expected.equivalent_output_rule: Any three traversable, well-spaced map-frame points inside the declared zone are acceptable.

reference_behavior: Derive three traversable points inside the north-zone polygon.
Navigate to all selected points.
Log temperature after each arrival.

missions.title: Husky bounded field coverage

complexity.label: complex
complexity.rationale: Coverage waypoints must be derived from a field polygon while respecting obstacles, boundary margin, and battery admission.

paraphrases.text: Cover the marked field with the Husky, stay inside its boundary, avoid the blocked patch, and only start with at least 20 percent battery.
Plan a ground coverage route for the whole field while respecting the boundary margin, mapped obstacle, and 20% battery minimum.
Explore the defined field using safe coverage waypoints that remain inside the polygon and outside the blocked area.

forbidden_assumptions: Do not claim that the current tree performs online frontier planning or runtime return-home behavior.

expected.equivalent_output_rule: An alternate route is acceptable if every segment stays in the inset polygon, avoids the blocked patch, and provides comparable field coverage.
expected.safety_note: Battery is an admission/review condition. The current XML does not implement a runtime low-battery or return-home guard.

reference_behavior: Check the current battery against the 20% admission threshold before approval.
Derive a coverage route in the map frame.
Keep route segments inside the inset boundary and outside the blocked patch.
Execute the fixed route; do not claim online frontier computation.

missions.title: BlueBoat context-derived shoreline temperature survey

complexity.label: complex
complexity.rationale: The route must be derived from shoreline geometry while satisfying interval, offset, geofence, exclusion-zone, and duration constraints.

paraphrases.text: Survey water temperature along the marked shoreline about every 20 metres, staying 10–20 m from shore and outside the reed protection zone.
Use the BlueBoat to profile shoreline temperature at roughly 20 m spacing without entering the exclusion area or leaving the water geofence.
Plan a shoreline water-temperature route that keeps the required shore offset, avoids protected reeds, and finishes within 45 minutes.

constraints: Keep the estimated mission duration within 45 minutes.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not place waypoints on land or inside the reed protection polygon.

expected.equivalent_output_rule: An alternate route is acceptable if samples remain approximately 20 m apart, 10–20 m from shore, inside the geofence, and outside the exclusion polygon.

reference_behavior: Derive water-safe waypoints from shoreline and geofence context.
Avoid the protected reed polygon while maintaining the allowed shore offset.
Navigate with BlueBoat and log temperature at each waypoint.
Reject or clarify if the constraints cannot be satisfied together.
missions.blocking_requirement: Implement and validate the BlueBoat capability model, navigation adapter, temperature interface, tree XML, metadata, and plan-review rules for water geometry.

missions.title: BlueBoat shoreline survey with one unavailable context source

complexity.label: complex
complexity.rationale: The route is context-derived under degraded satellite input, using independent OSM and local-chart evidence plus safety constraints.

paraphrases.text: Profile water temperature along the east shoreline every 25 metres; use the chart and OSM data because the satellite image is unavailable.
With no satellite map, plan the BlueBoat temperature route for the eastern shore from the remaining verified context.
Survey the east-shore sector at 25 m spacing using the local water chart and OSM shoreline, or ask if that evidence is insufficient.

constraints: Clarify if OSM and chart evidence disagree.
forbidden_assumptions: Do not describe the unavailable satellite map as observed or validated.

expected.equivalent_output_rule: A route based only on the agreeing OSM and local-chart shoreline is acceptable if it remains in the east sector, inside the geofence, and near 25 m spacing.

reference_behavior: Acknowledge that satellite context is unavailable.
Cross-check the agreeing OSM and local-chart shoreline evidence.
Derive east-sector water-safe waypoints at approximately 25 m spacing.
Navigate with BlueBoat and log temperature at each waypoint.
missions.blocking_requirement: Implement BlueBoat support and verify that the context pipeline represents unavailable sources without fabricating content.
